zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffrey de Warburton IV於大約1298年出生在英國英格蘭爾瓦布爾頓時，他的父親Geoffrey de Warburton III1歲，母親Alice De Wevers-3歲。他於大約1328年與Alice De Wevers結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1382年在英國爾瓦布爾頓享年86歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Warburton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place in Greater Manchester (formerly in Cheshire) called Warburton, from the Old English female personal name Wǣrburg (composed of the elements wǣr ‘pledge’ + burh ‘fortress’) + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Warbritton
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Warburton  . This form of the surname is not found in English sources.

variants.name: Warbington
meanings: English (Lancashire): perhaps a variant of   Warburton  ; otherwise a habitational name from a lost or unidentified place.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey de Warburton IV was born about 1298, in Warburton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Geoffrey de Warburton III, was 1 and his mother, Alice De Wevers, was -3. He married Alice De Wevers about 1328. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died about 1382, in Warburton, Cheshire, England, at the age of 86.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey de Warburton IV est né environ 1298, à Warburton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Geoffrey de Warburton III, avait 1 ans et sa mère, Alice De Wevers, avait -3 ans. Il a épousé Alice De Wevers environ 1328. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé environ 1382, à Warburton, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 86 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey de Warburton IV um 1298 in Warburton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Geoffrey de Warburton III 1 und seine Mutter Alice De Wevers -3. Er hat um 1328 Alice De Wevers geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb um 1382 im Alter von 86 Jahren in Warburton, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de Warburton IV è nato il circa 1298 nel Warburton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Geoffrey de Warburton III, aveva 1 anni e il suo madre, Alice De Wevers, aveva -3 anni. Lui sposato Alice De Wevers circa 1328. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì circa 1382, nel Warburton, Cheshire, England, all'età di 86 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Warburton IV(이)가 대략 1298년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 워버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffrey de Warburton III(은)는 1세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice De Wevers(은)는 -3세였다. 그는 대략 1328년 Alice De Wevers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1382년 영국 체실르 워버턴에서 86세의 나이로 사망했다.
